name: ELVET SHAFT & BORING

units: fathoms/feet/inches, transcribed verbatim — convert ONLY downstream

notes: Two-part record: shaft section i1–i29 (surface → 37-2-5), then 'Boring beneath Shaft beneath Hutton' brought forward from 33-4-1 (the Hutton base, NOT the shaft bottom) — boring interval i30 (measures Hutton→Harvey, 33-4-1 → 58-3-6) therefore overlaps and re-covers the shaft's lowest beds i27–i29 (33-4-1 → 37-2-5); downstream bed model must not double-count that 3-4-4 span. Page 1 header: 'SECTION OF South Engine Pit, Elvet Landsale Colliery' (colliery name scrawled; 'Elvet Landsale' is the best reading, matching the hole name and p3's 'strata sunk through in the S. Engine Pit, Elvet Lansdale Colliery'); 'Communicated by B⟨&?⟩S. 803' (p1), matching bold pencil 'B & S 803' on p3; date 1858; One-inch Map 27; Six-inch Durham 27 NW. Page 3 is a rougher working draft of the boring section with clerk arithmetic — all of it checks against the p2 fair copy (Harvey leaves 11″+10″ = 1′9″; below-Hutton Harvey depth 25-1-2 + 33-4-1 = 58-5-3; Busty group 8′4″; final 55-3-4 below Hutton + 33-4-1 = 89-1-5) — p2 preferred as the cleaner copy; only substantive disagreement is the lowest coal's label (p3 crossed-out 'Brockwell' vs p2 '?¾'), noted at i45. Page 4 is a blank ruled continuation sheet, no strata. Pencil marginalia include systematic OD conversions (180 − depth-in-feet: 95′→85 at Low Main, 148′→32 at TGT, 164′→16 at i20, Hutton 202′→−22 overlined) and seam correlations recorded in seam_marks. Clerk column arithmetic is CLEAN throughout — every depth equals previous depth + thickness, including the half-inch rows of the Busty group; no sum slips found. Bottom-of-page pencil scratch sums (p2: '84 1 …/82 1½…'; p3: '480/24/504/…') are working figures, not strata. Final depth 89-1-5 = 535 ft 5 in = 163.20 m (BGS index 163.08 m evidently converts 535 ft even, dropping the 5 inches).
